name: screen-reader-testing
description: Test web applications with screen readers including VoiceOver, NVDA, and JAWS. Use when validating screen reader compatibility, debugging accessibility issues, or ensuring assistive technology support.
qualityReason: Highly specific with clear tools and purpose
capabilities: Guide users through VoiceOver testing on macOS and iOS
Provide NVDA testing checklists and keyboard commands
Show JAWS and TalkBack essential shortcuts
Explain screen reader modes (Browse/Virtual and Focus/Forms)
Provide code patterns for accessible modals and live regions
List common accessibility issues with fixes
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 61 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a documentation/guide for screen reader accessibility testing. The scanner detected markdown formatting characters (backticks in code blocks, tildes in tables) and legitimate documentation URLs, misinterpreting them as security risks. No actual code execution, credential access, or malicious network activity exists.
valueStatement: Ensure your web applications work with screen readers like VoiceOver, NVDA, and JAWS. This skill provides testing checklists, keyboard commands, and code patterns for accessible development.
limitations: Cannot actually run screen readers - provides guidance only
Does not test automated accessibility (use axe-core separately)
Does not replace manual testing with actual screen readers

useCases.title: Accessibility Audit
useCases.description: Conduct a comprehensive screen reader compatibility audit before launching a new web application.

useCases.title: Code Review for Accessibility
useCases.description: Review frontend code to ensure ARIA attributes and semantic HTML support screen reader users.

useCases.title: Learn Screen Reader Commands
useCases.description: Get quick reference guides for VoiceOver, NVDA, JAWS, and TalkBack keyboard navigation.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Screen Reader Test
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me test my web page with screen readers. What are the key things to check for VoiceOver on macOS?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to start screen reader testing

promptTemplates.title: NVDA Testing Guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a testing script for NVDA on Windows. What keyboard commands should I use to navigate forms and check for proper labeling?

promptTemplates.title: Fix Accessibility Issues
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a modal dialog that screen readers cannot access properly. Show me the correct ARIA implementation with focus management.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has specific accessibility bug to fix

promptTemplates.title: Live Region Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to use aria-live regions to announce dynamic content updates to screen reader users. Give code examples.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to implement dynamic content announcements

bestPractices: Test with actual screen readers, not just browser simulators
Always test both browse mode and focus/forms mode
Verify focus management when opening and closing modals
Use semantic HTML before adding ARIA attributes
antiPatterns: Relying solely on automated accessibility testing tools
Using aria-label when visible text is available
Ignoring focus order in single-page applications

faq.question: Which screen readers should I test with?
faq.answer: Minimum: NVDA+Firefox (Windows) and VoiceOver+Safari (macOS). Comprehensive: add JAWS+Chrome, TalkBack+Android, and Narrator+Edge.

faq.question: What is the difference between Browse mode and Focus mode?
faq.answer: Browse mode uses arrow keys for content navigation. Focus mode uses arrow keys for widget control. NVDA auto-switches when entering forms or ARIA applications.

faq.question: How do I test dynamic content updates?
faq.answer: Use aria-live regions. Set aria-live='polite' for non-critical updates, aria-live='assertive' for urgent alerts. Test that content changes are announced.

faq.question: What is focus trapping and why does it matter?
faq.answer: Focus trapping keeps keyboard focus inside a modal or dialog until it closes. This prevents screen reader users from accidentally navigating behind the modal.

faq.question: Can I test screen readers in CI/CD?
faq.answer: Automated tools like axe-core can catch many issues but cannot replace manual screen reader testing. Use CI for automated checks, manual testing for the full experience.

faq.question: What is the VoiceOver modifier key?
faq.answer: The VoiceOver modifier (VO) is Ctrl+Option. All VoiceOver commands use this combination. For example, VO+Right Arrow moves to the next element.
